code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test24-MAY-2024Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, indicating impaired renal function. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, glucosuria, low specific gravity, and mild ketonuria, with no evidence of infection (negative nitrites/leukocytes). These findings suggest possible diabetic nephropathy or another form of chronic kidney disease. Further nephrological evaluation and assessment of glycemic control are recommended.

coding.display: Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
